accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and verified statute (Section 546 of the Public Health Service Act, the first responder training program).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; contextual identification of the program and statute is accurate and verified.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill reauthorizes and modifies the first responder training program under Section 546 of the PHSA.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly describes the specific amendments to Section 546 of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 290ee-1).

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill contains no material exceptions or carve-outs beyond the amending edits, which are covered.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the fiscal years 2026-2030 authorization period and that no separate effective date is specified.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures all essential operative effects: broadening beyond opioids, the 'legally marketed' expansion, the drug-type expansions, and reauthorization.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized and internally consistent, walking through each amendment logically.

concise.why: Free of padding or repetition while covering all provisions.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names Section 546 of the Public Health Service Act and the first responder training program.

correct_figures.why: The $36 million (FY2019-2023) and $57 million (FY2026-2030) figures match the bill text exactly.
